title: This New England town hosts one of America’s best 4th of July celebrations, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Bristol, R.I. ranks fifth in USA Today's list of top 10 Fourth of July celebrations in the U.S.
The Bristol Fourth of July Celebration, starting in 1785, is the oldest continuous celebration in the country.
USA Today's 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards involved selections by travel experts and voting by the publication's readers.

title: England use a Beatles soundtrack to reveal controversial World Cup squad picks
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: England announced their World Cup squad using a Beatles-themed video featuring the song 'Come Together.'
Notable omissions from the squad include Trent Alexander-Arnold, Harry Maguire, Cole Palmer, and Phil Foden.
Surprise selections include Ivan Toney and Djed Spence, despite the latter's recent injury.

title: Stephen Colbert bids farewell to ‘Late Show’ with star-studded finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Stephen Colbert hosted the final episode of 'The Late Show' with Paul McCartney as the last guest.
Celebrity appearances included Bryan Cranston, Paul Rudd, Tig Notaro, Tim Meadows, and Ryan Reynolds.
The finale featured a surreal segment with a wormhole and ended with musical performances by Elvis Costello and Paul McCartney.

title: Head to these fan favorite beach towns this Memorial Day weekend
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Ogunquit, Maine was voted New England's best beach town in Boston.com's Battle of the Beach Towns.
JetBlue will end its service at Manchester-Boston Regional Airport effective July 8.
Harwich was named the favorite Cape Cod town by Boston.com readers, receiving over 850 votes.

title: WWII-era rockets found in Nantucket sand dune
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: WWII-era rockets discovered in a Nantucket sand dune were identified as unexploded military ordnance from training exercises.
Massachusetts State Police Bomb Squad neutralized the hazardous rockets with a controlled explosion on the island.
Local and state officials took over the scene after ensuring the area was safe, authorities confirmed.

title: 18-year-old allegedly brandishes gun during argument outside Mass. high school prom
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old allegedly brandished a gun during a confrontation outside a South Shore high school prom in Kingston.
Police reported the suspect threatened to run over a student before fleeing the scene; no injuries occurred.
The suspect, not a student, was apprehended at his home in Randolph and is in custody; investigation ongoing.

title: Car camping is trending this season, according to Hipcamp
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Hipcamp reports a surge in car camping popularity, with over 1,000 related searches in Massachusetts.
Car camping involves sleeping in a car at campsites with easy access, privacy, and nearby amenities.
Hipcamp added a 'car camping' filter due to rising interest, ranking it fourth among rig filters.

title: 1 killed in Dorchester shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A person was fatally shot near 260 Bowdoin St. in Dorchester around 1 a.m. Friday.
The victim was transported to a hospital but was pronounced dead shortly after arrival.
Boston Police have not released the victim's identity or information on any arrests; investigation is ongoing.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge on recent Greater Boston news.
The quiz covers trending stories in the Greater Boston area for the week.
Peter Chianca, General Assignment Editor since 2019, oversees quiz content.

title: How well do you know your World Cup trivia? Take our quiz.
author: Hayden Bird and Kelly Chan
bullets: Gillette Stadium will host its first 2026 World Cup match in a few weeks.
A quiz tests readers' knowledge of World Cup history and trivia.
Eric Wynalda scored against Switzerland in the 1994 World Cup at the Pontiac Silverdome.
